identity.canonical_title: MUTUAL INFORMATION FUNCTIONAL Canonical Statutory Formula: I(A;B;C)

theory.canonical_statement: [ID 1087] MUTUAL INFORMATION FUNCTIONAL Canonical Statutory Formula: $I(A;B;C) = \sum S(X_i) - S(total)$ Formal Technical Dissertation: Formalizes mutual information for the tripartite system. It governs correlation between channels, ensuring thermodynamic consistency and identifying emergent information integration. Source: [56]
theory.technical_interpretation: MUTUAL INFORMATION FUNCTIONAL Canonical Statutory Formula: I(A;B;C) is a series / summation, written I(A;B;C) = ∑ S(X_i) - S(total). It is expressed as a sum or series over modes or indices, superposing discrete contributions. It is built from S, X_i, o, a, l, defining I(A;B;C). Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
